step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning**: Multiple sources confirm that H3K27M mutations are a defining feature of diffuse midline gliomas, including diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma (DIPG), which are classified under the 2016 WHO guidelines specifically as "diffuse midline glioma, H3K27-mutant." While the exact histone variant (H3.1 vs. H3.2 vs. H3.3) may differ, the mutation occurs across variants and leads to identical epigenetic effects—loss of H3K27me3—and drives tumorigenesis regardless of origin. Therefore, H3.3K27M is recognized as part of this broader category of H3K27M-mutant gliomas.
